description: P2 - Refactor Decisions, Workflows, Workflow Detail, Graph, Login, and Auth pages: eliminate inline styles, add shared classes, fix Login page (oauth btn class, terms links, spinner loading states), fix Settings/Advanced back link accent
steps: In osai-web/app/decisions/page.tsx: (a) Replace inline style={{fontSize:12, padding:'6px 14px'}} on filter buttons with btn-sm class. (b) Replace inline style={{fontSize:11, padding:'3px 7px'}} on action buttons with btn-xs class. (c) Remove other inline style overrides (marginBottom, gap, flexWrap, etc) by adding utility classes to globals.css where needed or using existing ones.

In osai-web/app/workflows/page.tsx: (a) Replace the expand caret characters ('▲'/'▼') with lucide ChevronDown/ChevronUp icons. (b) Replace inline style on the RunCard button (width:100%, background:none, border:none, padding, cursor, textAlign, display:flex) with a .workflow-run-header class in globals.css. (c) Replace inline padding overrides on btn elements with btn-sm class. (d) Replace emoji 👤 with lucide User icon.

In osai-web/app/workflows/[id]/page.tsx: (a) Replace the inline grid style (gridTemplateColumns: 'repeat(auto-fill, minmax(160px, 1fr))') in the meta card with a class `.detail-meta-grid` in globals.css. (b) Add a `.breadcrumb` class to globals.css (font-size:12px, color:var(--text-muted), margin-bottom:20px) and use it. (c) Replace inline padding overrides on buttons with btn-sm class. (d) Replace emoji 👤 with lucide User icon. (e) Add Loader2 spinner loading state: import Loader2 from lucide-react, replace the plain text 'Loading workflow run...' with a centered spinner animation.

In osai-web/app/graph/page.tsx: (a) Replace inline tier badge styles (the display:inline-flex with padding, borderRadius:9999, fontSize:11, etc.) with a reusable class `.tier-badge` and modifier classes `.tier-badge--normal`, `.tier-badge--amber`, `.tier-badge--red` in globals.css. (b) Replace inline legend dot styles with CSS classes.

In osai-web/app/login/page.tsx: (a) Add a `.login-oauth-btn` class to globals.css for the Google button (display:flex, align-items:center, justify-content:center, gap:10px, cursor:pointer, font-weight:600, margin-bottom:14px) and replace the inline styles on the Google button. (b) Make the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy text in the footer actual links: wrap them in <a href='#'> tags so they are clickable (can be # hrefs).

In osai-web/app/auth/callback/page.tsx: (a) Replace inline styles with CSS classes. Add a `.auth-callback-wrapper` class to globals.css (min-height:100vh, display:flex, flex-direction:column, align-items:center, justify-content:center, background:var(--bg), gap:16px, color:var(--text-secondary)). (b) Add Loader2 spinner: import from lucide-react, add a spinning Loader2 icon alongside the text.

In osai-web/app/settings/advanced/page.tsx: Change the back link to use accent color: add `style={{ color: 'var(--accent)' }}` or better, add a `.back-link` class to globals.css (color: var(--accent), display:inline-flex, align-items:center, gap:4px, font-size:12px, text-decoration:none, margin-bottom:12px) and use that class instead of the .meta class.
acceptance_criteria: Decisions page buttons use btn-sm and btn-xs classes
Workflows page uses ChevronDown/ChevronUp icons instead of text arrows
Workflow Detail has a .detail-meta-grid class and .breadcrumb class
Workflow Detail loading state shows a Loader2 spinner
Auth Callback page has spinner and uses CSS class instead of inline styles
Graph page tier badges use CSS classes instead of inline styles
Login page Google button uses .login-oauth-btn class
Login page Terms/Privacy are actual <a> links
Settings/Advanced back link uses accent color
No emoji 👤 remains in workflows or workflow detail pages
npm run typecheck and npm run build pass
findings: All acceptance criteria met. Typecheck and build pass cleanly. animate-spin utility is provided by tw-animate-css package (imported in theme.css). The @keyframes spin is also defined in globals.css as a fallback. No remaining inline style violations in the modified pages. No emoji icons remain in workflows pages.
